title: Glossary
text:
Term
Meaning




Report
One observed/suspected bloom event.


Event
The first-class record a report becomes; some events become cases.


Case
A grouping of related reports for a waterbody (one region, one year).


Response / Advisory
A staff action; an advisory is the public-facing caution/warning/danger.


Determination / outcome
What a report turned out to be.


Submission
A public/community report awaiting staff review (not yet a tracked report).


Promote
Turn a pending submission into a tracked report.


Provisional data
Live data from this system, not the official data.ca.gov release.


CEDEN
The state environmental data exchange (lab results format).


Batch
One ingest of lab data (an email folder, a CEDEN file, or a template) that samples are grouped under.


CoC (chain of custody)
The form tracking a sample from field to lab; often where a sample’s coordinates are recorded.


Sample / result
A sample is one collected specimen; a result is one analyte value measured on it.


Station
A sampling location (often a CEDEN station code); geocoded to a point.


Geocode
Give a sample a map location (from the station registry or coordinates off the CoC).


Reconcile / link
Connect a lab sample to the event/report/case it belongs to.


Routine sampling
Monitoring not tied to a bloom report/case; tagged so it leaves the unfinished queue.


Non-detect (ND)
Analyte tested for but not detected above the method limit.


HUC-12 / GeoConnex
Watershed coding / persistent identifiers for locations.

title: Ingesting lab data
text: The central goal of this pilot is getting cyanotoxin lab chemistry into FHAB and connected to the right event / report / case. Lab results arrive in several shapes — an email folder from Bend Genetics, a CEDEN export, a one-off spreadsheet for a single site — so there are a few ways in. This page walks each one, then the reconciliation step that links the data to events.

title: Ingesting lab data
section: Pick the path that matches how the data arrived
text:
Pick the path that matches how the data arrived








The lab data arrived as…
Use
Menu




A folder of email attachments from Bend/partner (a results spreadsheet + chain-of-custody / transmittal / receipt PDFs)
Ingest lab email folders
Ingest Data → Ingest lab email folders


A CEDEN WaterChemistry CSV (the Bend→CEDEN workflow output)
Upload CEDEN lab data
Ingest Data → Upload CEDEN lab data


A full CEDEN chemistry template where StationCode + date is the only link to an event
Lab batch reconciliation
Ingest Data → Lab batch reconciliation


Results for one already-known report or case
Attach on the report/case
Reports / Cases → Lab upload



All paths land the samples where you can reconcile them (link to events) on the Lab data workboard. Start with the workflow that matches your file; the rest of the page is the same downstream.

title: Ingesting lab data
section: A. Email folder upload (Bend / partner) — the newest path
text:
A. Email folder upload (Bend / partner) — the newest path
Labs typically email a folder: a wide-format results spreadsheet (analytes across columns) plus a scanned chain-of-custody (CoC), a transmittal letter, and a sample-receipt form. This path converts that spreadsheet, brings the samples in, and keeps the original files on the batch for provenance.
Steps

Ingest Data → Ingest lab email folders.
Under Folder files, select all the files from the one email folder (the results CSV/XLSX and the PDFs). Add a Source label like Clear Lake (RB5) (optional but helpful).
Ingest folder. You’ll get a summary — “Ingested N samples (M geocoded), K results; stored J files” — and land on the workboard filtered to that batch.







TipMany folders at once



Have a parent folder that holds many lab folders (one per email)? Use the Many folders at once box on the same screen: pick the parent folder and Ingest all subfolders. Each subfolder becomes its own batch (labeled with the subfolder name), uploaded one at a time with a running progress line — so a 50-folder set won’t overload a single request. The tally at the end names any folders that failed so you can retry just those.


What happens

The wide spreadsheet is converted to CEDEN long form: each analyte value becomes a result (ELISA toxins, dry-weight toxins, qPCR genes, chlorophyll). ND becomes a proper non-detect.
Samples come in unlinked and are geocoded from the CEDEN station registry when the station code is known. Samples whose station isn’t in the registry come in ungeocoded — their coordinates usually live on the CoC (see Geocoding).
The source files stay on the batch: on the workboard batch banner (and the Ingested batches table) you’ll see coc · data · receipt · transmittal links — they open in a new tab so your workspace stays put.

What to test

Upload a folder; do the sample/result/geocoded counts look right?
Open the stored CoC link — does it open in a new tab and show the scanned form?
A folder with no results spreadsheet (only PDFs) should still store the files.
Do the samples appear on the workboard, filtered to the new batch?

title: Ingesting lab data
section: B. Upload CEDEN lab data (workflow output)
text:
B. Upload CEDEN lab data (workflow output)
For a CEDEN WaterChemistry CSV (the output of the Bend→CEDEN workflow), use Ingest Data → Upload CEDEN lab data. You can upload a file or pull from a URL. This loads samples + results, geocodes stations from the registry, and auto-links samples to nearby events where it’s confident.
What to test: upload a CEDEN chemistry CSV; confirm samples/results load and that obvious station+date matches auto-link (the rest wait on the workboard).

title: Ingesting lab data
section: C. Lab batch reconciliation (CEDEN template, staged)
text:
C. Lab batch reconciliation (CEDEN template, staged)
For a full CEDEN chemistry template where you want to review every link before it’s written, use Ingest Data → Lab batch reconciliation.

Upload the template CSV. Optionally widen the match radius (m) and ± days window.
The batch is staged — nothing touches live reports yet.
For each station + date group, the matcher suggests candidate reports within the windows. Promote a confident match, link manually, or create a report from the station.
Use Auto-link to accept all high-confidence, unambiguous matches at once, then review the rest.

What to test: stage a template; confirm nothing is linked until you act; try Auto-link, then link one group manually and create a report from another.

title: Ingesting lab data
section: D. Attach results to one report or case
text:
D. Attach results to one report or case
When results belong to a single known site, skip matching entirely: open the report (or case) and use its Lab upload to attach a CEDEN CSV directly. Every sample pins to that report/case.

title: Ingesting lab data
section: After ingest: reconcile on the Lab data workboard
text:
After ingest: reconcile on the Lab data workboard
Ingest Data → Lab data workboard is where lab samples get connected to events/reports/cases and quality-reviewed. It’s built as a task board: the lead assigns work, team members link, a reviewer QA-approves or flags.

Status chips: Unlinked, Linked · awaiting QA, Flagged for rework, QA approved, and Routine sampling, plus Geocoded · not linked and Needs geocoding — click one to filter. The last two split the unlinked pile into samples that are ready to research (they have a location) versus those still waiting on coordinates.
Filters: status, assignee (incl. Me / Unassigned), region, a Geocoding filter (Geocoded / Not geocoded), and a station / water-body search. A batch link filters the board to just that ingest.
Assign: check rows, pick a team member under “With checked rows →”, Assign.
Per sample: Link / Re-link by report (R#) or case (C#); +Report (create one from the station); ✓ QA approve; ⚑ flag with a reason; Unlink; Routine (see below). Re-linking clears QA so the sample returns to the review queue.
Batch reconcile: Reconcile checked or Reconcile all unlinked in this view auto-links the confident, unambiguous matches in one pass.


The sample map (🗺) — the research popup
Click a sample’s 🗺 to open its context map. This is where the tedious research/linking happens, so it gathers everything in one place:

A provenance summary: how the sample was ingested (Email folder ingest — , CEDEN chemistry upload, Lab batch reconciliation, data.ca.gov / legacy import), its lab identity (station, type, BG_ID, lab sample id, lab batch, project), result count, and its source files (coc · data · transmittal …, opening in a new tab) — or “no source files stored for this ingest” when there aren’t any.
The map: the sample as a ★, and nearby reports colored by how close in time they are to the sample (blue ≤14 d, yellow 14–30 d, pink 30–60 d, gray >60 d). Click a marker to link to that report or its case, or open the report in a new tab.
A list of the nearby reports below the map (same reports as the markers) — handy when markers overlap and are hard to click. Check one to link the sample to that report, or check several that share a case and Link selected to link the sample to that case at once.



Geocoding ungeocoded samples
Samples whose station isn’t in the registry come in without a location — their coordinates are usually on the CoC. On the sample map you can:

Open the batch’s CoC (new tab) and read the latitude/longitude.
Type them into the lat / long boxes → Search nearby to preview reports/cases around that point (shown as a ◆).
Save to sample to store the coordinates — the sample is now geocoded, appears on the main map, and becomes eligible for batch reconcile.
Scan CoC (OCR) will try to read the coordinates off the CoC automatically. In the current hosted pilot this is a best-effort helper and may report that OCR isn’t available — if so, just read the CoC and type the numbers.

There are also two batch-level ways to enter coordinates:

Per-sample entry screen — when a batch has several samples that each have their own coordinates on the CoC (often under IDs different from the lab’s sample IDs). From the Ingested batches table (Enter coords) or the workboard batch banner (Enter sample coordinates), open a screen that lists every sample in the batch — station, customer / CoC label, lab sample id, BG_ID, date, type — with a latitude/longitude box each, and the CoC linked at top. Transcribe each sample’s coordinates and Save. Each sample gets its own point, so samples the ingest put on one station are split apart correctly.
Bulk paste — Ingest Data → Bulk sample coordinates: paste one row per line as station code, latitude, longitude (codes with spaces/commas are fine — the last two numbers are taken as lat/long). Matching a station geocodes every sample at it.



Routine sampling (not every sample is a bloom report)
Some samples are routine monitoring, not tied to any bloom report or case. On an unlinked sample, use Routine (row button or the map popup’s Tag routine sampling) to move it into the Routine sampling bucket so it stops showing as unfinished work. ↩︎ Unlinked puts it back. (Routine sub-types will come later.)

title: Ingesting lab data
text:
Suggested lab-ingest test script (~15 min)

Ingest an email folder (Ingest Data → Ingest lab email folders) — pick all files from one lab folder, add a source label, ingest. Check the counts and open the stored CoC (new tab). (Or try Many folders at once with a parent folder of several subfolders.)
On the workboard, use the Geocoded · not linked chip; open a sample’s 🗺 and read its provenance summary (how it was ingested + files), then link it to a nearby report from the list — or +Report to create one.
Use the Needs geocoding chip to find an ungeocoded sample; read its coordinates off the CoC, type them in, Search nearby, then Save to sample. For a batch with several samples, try the batch’s Enter coords screen instead.
Tag one sample as Routine sampling, then confirm it appears under the Routine chip.
Run Reconcile all unlinked in this view and see how many auto-link.
Open Lab (results browser) and confirm your batch’s results appear; Download CSV.
Note anything confusing along the way — see Giving feedback.

title: Feedback & limitations
text:
For each issue, please tell us:

Which app (CyanoSafe phone / desktop / FHAB Staff) and which page.
What you did, what you expected, and what happened.
A screenshot if you can, and the date/time and your browser/device.
Severity: blocker / annoying / cosmetic / suggestion.

Send feedback to [contact provided separately]. There’s no in-app bug button yet.

title: Feedback & limitations
text:
For each issue, please tell us:

Which app (CyanoSafe phone / desktop / FHAB Staff) and which page.
What you did, what you expected, and what happened.
A screenshot if you can, and the date/time and your browser/device.
Severity: blocker / annoying / cosmetic / suggestion.

Send feedback to [contact provided separately]. There’s no in-app bug button yet.

title: Feedback & limitations
section: Known limitations (no need to report these)
text:
Known limitations (no need to report these)
These are known and on the roadmap — please don’t file them as bugs:

No email yet by default — notifications are in-app (the 🔔 bell). Email escalation is only active if the program configures SMTP.
No password reset / SSO yet — if you’re locked out, an admin resets your login.
No full audit history yet (only a light “recent activity” log).
Publishing to data.ca.gov is manual — the app generates the files; uploading them is a manual/scheduled step.
GeoConnex IDs are shown but not yet publicly resolvable.
The free hosting tier can be slow on the first request after idle (it “wakes up”); give it a few seconds.

title: Program staff
text: You’ll log in to the FHAB Staff app at https://fhab-web.onrender.com.

title: Program staff
text:
Roles
What you can see and do depends on your role.







Role
Can do




program_admin
Everything, incl. account management and community-group API keys


wb_staff
Enter/edit reports, review submissions, cases, advisories, lab data


field_staff
Field verification + results entry


lab_analyst
Lab results / CEDEN data


illness_workgroup
Receives suspected-illness escalations


ddw_staff
Drinking-water focus



Your data is scoped to your Regional Water Board — you generally see and manage your region’s records. (Cross-region report entry is allowed with a confirmation, for filing on behalf of another board.)

title: Program staff
text:
First login

Go to https://fhab-web.onrender.com and sign in with the credentials provided.
You land on the Dashboard: quick actions, and the reports you’ve recently worked on.
Note the 🔔 bell (top right) — your notifications, including new submissions and illness alerts.

title: Program staff
text:
The main things to test

1. Review the submission queue (Submissions)

New community/app reports land here as pending.
For each: choose a region and Promote (creates a tracked report) or Reject.
Promoting carries over everything the reporter sent (location, bloom details, photo, and any suspected-illness info) and starts the report as under investigation.
Try the filters (pending / promoted / rejected) and the trusted groups filter.



2. Enter a new report (New report)

Mirrors the official MyWaterQuality bloom form: report type, water body, county, landmark, coordinates, bloom characteristics (size, textures, weather, signs), reporter contact, suspected illness, and photos.
Water body has a type-ahead that suggests existing waterbodies — pick one to avoid duplicates; a near-duplicate name prompts a confirm.
County is a controlled dropdown.



3. Open a report (Reports → click one)

Edit field-verification details and set the outcome / determination.
Add field/lab results, or upload a CEDEN lab CSV.
Record a response / advisory (an advisory with “display on map” drives the public map).
Staff-only: reporter contact and the suspected illness/death matrix.



4. Explore the map (Map)

The map plots reports, colored by outcome. Use the filters to narrow it:

Region, County, Outcome, and Advisory dropdowns.
Date-range buttons — 15 / 30 / 60 / 120 days (or All) — by observation date.
Analytical data modes: Events with lab data, or Geocoded, not linked (geocoded lab samples not yet linked or tagged routine — teal markers at their station, with a Reconcile link that opens the workboard filtered to the geocoded-unlinked queue).

Filters apply server-side, so they reach the whole dataset. Click a marker for the report popup; Clear all resets everything.



5. Cases (Cases)

Group related reports into a case (one region, one waterbody, one year); assign reports, set status/lead, and upload case-level lab data.



6. Lab data — ingest & reconcile (Ingest Data)
Bringing lab chemistry in and connecting it to events is the focus of this round and has its own page: ★ Ingesting lab data. In brief, under Ingest Data you can:

Ingest lab email folders — a Bend/partner folder (results spreadsheet + CoC/transmittal PDFs); the files are kept on the batch.
Upload CEDEN lab data — a CEDEN WaterChemistry CSV (auto-links confident matches).
Lab batch reconciliation — a staged CEDEN template you link group-by-group.
Lab data workboard — assign / link / QA-review, geocode from the CoC, and tag routine sampling.
Bulk sample coordinates — paste station, lat, lon rows to geocode many at once.

See the dedicated page for step-by-step instructions and a lab-ingest test script.


7. Lab results browser (Lab)

Browse all field and lab results across reports in one place. Filter by water body / analyte (search), analysis type, data type, region, sample-date range, and non-detects; sort by date, water body, analyte, or value; and Download CSV of the filtered set.



8. Notifications (🔔)

Confirm you receive a notice when a new submission comes in (and an ⚠ illness alert if one reports suspected illness). “Mark all read” clears the badge.



9. Open data (Open data)

Download the published flat files (CSV or a zip), or view the provisional JSON API. There are six datasets: the four FHAB files, plus CEDEN Chemistry Results (analyte results in the statewide CEDEN structure) and a crosswalk that links each chemistry result to its watershed/GeoConnex and to the FHAB report/case.
Confirm these contain no reporter contact / illness / veterinary data.



10. Admin only — Accounts, Groups, Analytes, Refresh & Reset

Accounts: create users and grant/revoke roles.
Groups: register a community/partner group and mint an API key (shown once) so that group can submit attributed — and optionally “trusted” — reports.
Analytes: curate the analyte vocabulary — edit an analyte’s name/class/unit, merge aliases (e.g. “mcyE” → Microcystins) so results consolidate, and delete unused ones.
Refresh from data.ca.gov: pull the latest published reports, cases, responses, and results and update the schema. Always Preview first (a dry run shows new / updated per dataset); it inserts new records and refreshes published fields on existing ones, preserves local-only data (illness, assignments, QA, lab links), and never deletes. Type UPDATE to apply.
Refresh / Reset (test environment): purge lab data to reset the pilot.

title: Program staff
text:
A suggested 15-minute test script

Submit a test report from the CyanoSafe phone app (Community track) — include a suspected illness.
Log in to the Staff app; confirm the 🔔 bell shows new + illness notifications.
Go to Submissions, Promote your test report to your region.
Open the new report; set an outcome, add a field result, and post an advisory.
Create a Case and assign the report to it.
Open the Map; filter by your region and the last 30 days, and find your report.
Open Lab and confirm your field result shows in the cross-report results browser.
Go to Open data; download bloom-report.csv; confirm your report is in it and that no reporter name/illness columns appear.
Note anything confusing along the way.

title: Community tester
text: You don’t need an account. You’re testing the “Report a Bloom” flow and confirming a submission reaches staff.

title: Community tester
section: Submit a test bloom report (phone / PWA)
text:
Submit a test bloom report (phone / PWA)

On your phone, open the CyanoSafe phone app. (Optional: use your browser’s “Add to Home Screen” to install it like an app.)
Tap Bloom List (bottom), then expand 📋 Report a Bloom.
(Optional) Tap 📷 Take / Choose Photo — this also captures your GPS location.
Fill in at least the Water body name (required) and a location (your GPS, or pick a County). Add date, bloom size, and a short description if you can.
Tap Submit report. You should see “Thank you — your report was received for review.”







Tip



Please put TEST in the description (e.g. “TEST – ignore”) so staff know it’s a drill.








WarningUse a safe email



This is a test system holding only open or demo data. If you provide an email, use a work or publicly available address — not a private/sensitive one — and don’t enter any PII you wouldn’t want disclosed. See the data & privacy disclaimer.

title: Community tester
text:
What happens next

Your report does not appear on the public map immediately. It goes into a staff review queue, where a staffer promotes it to a tracked report or rejects it.
If you reported a suspected illness (you or an animal got sick), that automatically alerts the program’s illness team.

title: Community tester
text:
What to test / look for

Does the form submit successfully? Does the thank-you message appear?
Does 📷 + location work on your phone? Is the GPS roughly right?
Try submitting with only a water body name + county (no GPS). Does it still work?
Try an obviously bad entry (no water body name). Do you get a clear error?
Is anything confusing, mislabeled, or hard to tap?

title: FHAB Database Modernized — Tester Guide
text: Welcome, and thank you for helping test the new FHAB system. This guide is for program staff and community volunteers. Please report anything confusing or broken — see Giving feedback.

title: FHAB Database Modernized — Tester Guide
text:
Pick your track

★ Ingesting lab data — the priority for this round: bring lab chemistry in (email folders, CEDEN, spreadsheets) and reconcile it to events/reports/cases.
Program staff — the rest of the staff workspace: submissions, reports, cases, map, open data, admin.
Community tester — submit a bloom report from the CyanoSafe app, no login needed.

title: FHAB Database Modernized — Tester Guide
text:
Systems and links



System
What it is
Link




CyanoSafe map (desktop)
Public HAB map + “Report a Bloom” form
Open


CyanoSafe app (phone / PWA)
The mobile app most community users will use
Open


FHAB Staff app
Staff workspace (login required)
Open









Note



Your staff login and any community-group API key are provided separately — they are not in this guide.
